## Logical packet

This is a logical packet that is shipped as `total_frags`
fragments on the `UDP S->C 0x03/0x07` multipart channel. The
catalog row counts logical CharInfo deliveries, not individual
fragments. Sizes in the evidence block above (1357-4130 bytes)
are the reassembled body sizes; fragment sizes (73-1011 bytes)
are reported separately for MTU analysis.

## Per-fragment wire layout

Verified against retail captures + Ceres-J's
[`PacketBuilderUDP130307.emitMultipart`](../../../src/main/java/server/networktools/PacketBuilderUDP130307.java).
Each fragment carries an 11-byte header followed by a slice of
the body:

```
Offset  Size  Field          Notes
0x00    2     frag_idx       LE2 — 0..total_frags-1
0x02    4     total_frags    LE4 — number of fragments in this chain
0x06    1     discriminator  = 0x01 for CharInfo
0x07    3     data_size      LE3 — total reassembled body size
0x0a    1     chain_key      = 0x00 for the login chain;
                              other values for runtime chains
0x0b    var   fragment data  this fragment's slice of the body
```

The chain is identified by `chain_key`. Login uses
`chain_key=0x00`; subsequent runtime chains MUST use a different
key (see [feedback memory: multipart_framing](../../../../../../home/javier/.claude/projects/-home-javier-Documents-Projects-Neocron/memory/multipart_framing.md)).

## Reassembled body layout

After all fragments are concatenated in `frag_idx` order, the
reassembled body begins:

```
Offset  Size  Field
0x00    1     0x22       — multipart prelude marker
0x01    1     0x02       — section-stream version?
0x02    1     0x01       — corresponds to discriminator
0x03    var   sections   — TLV stream (CharInfo Sections 1..8)
```

Sections are TLV: `[id u8][size LE2][body]`. See
[`charinfo_byte_map.md`](../../charinfo_byte_map.md) (legacy) for
section-by-section content. Section meanings:

| Section | Content |
|---|---|
| 1 | Identity (name, slot, faction, …) |
| 2 | Pools (HP, PSI, STA — current + max) |
| 3 | Sympathies / clan / soullight |
| 4 | Subskills (45×(value,rank) pairs) — see `charinfo_s4_subskill_table.md` |
| 5 | Inventory metadata |
| 6 | Inventory contents |
| 7 | Skills (parent skills, derived) |
| 8 | Money (CASH @ S8+1, u32le) + misc |

## Reassembler behavior (Ghidra-verified)

From `FUN_0055c270` decompile:

1. Reassembler keys on `chain_key`. **Login uses `chain_key=0x00`**;
   any subsequent multipart that wants to start a fresh chain
   MUST use a different `chain_key` — otherwise fragments append
   silently to the closed login chain.
2. On full-buffer reassembly, the dispatcher branches on
   `discriminator`. For `disc=0x01` (this packet):
   - "CharInfo received" → fires UI event `0xa7` (size `0x45`) to
     the WORLDCLIENT-managed UI dispatcher at `this+0x10c`.
   - Sets bit 0 of `this+0x2ae`.
   - **Has a guard:** only fires the event the FIRST time bit 0
     is unset → subsequent `disc=0x01` multiparts do nothing.
3. After firing, calls `FUN_0055ce20` which sets the "ready"
   flag at `this+0x190` only when `(this[0x2ae] & 0xf) == 0xf`
   (all 4 low bits set). Bits 0 (this packet) and 1 (CharsysInfo,
   `disc=0x02`) are documented; bits 2 and 3 are unidentified.

## Observed contexts

Single CharInfo delivery during world entry of veteran
characters (body > ~900 bytes). Fresh characters take the
single-packet `0x03/0x2c` path — see
[`udp_s2c_03_2c.md`](udp_s2c_03_2c.md).

The corpus has 7 logical deliveries across 7 captures. Each
shows `chain_key=0x00`, consistent with the login-chain rule
(no runtime CharInfo redelivery observed in any capture).

## Open questions

- The login-time guard means `disc=0x01` only fires UI event
  `0xa7` ONCE per session. How does retail push CharInfo updates
  mid-session? (e.g. after a level-up that adds skill points.)
  Is it via `disc=0x02` CharsysInfo only, or via a different
  channel entirely?
- What sets bits 2 and 3 of `this+0x2ae`?

## Server-side handler

Builder side:
[`server/networktools/PacketBuilderUDP130307.java`](../../../src/main/java/server/networktools/PacketBuilderUDP130307.java)
(method `emitMultipart`).

Reassembly is purely client-side; no server reassembler.
